task_description: Create a new code repository with a name and description, initialize it with a README, and configure a .gitignore template and an Apache 2.0 license.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the semitransparent red X centered on the long rounded Google search input box, specifically on the blank text-entry area beneath the multicolored Google logo. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open to a new tab page in Chinese, with the Google homepage centered, the search box in the middle, shortcut icons below it, and the browser already focused.
caption.think: The user likely wants to place the text cursor into the Google search field so they can begin entering a search query.
caption.action: Click the Google search box in the center of the page.
caption.expectation: The search field should become active and show a blinking text cursor, ready for typing.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in view centers on the active Google search field, a rounded white input box beneath the multicolored Google logo. The text currently being entered is "github," shown in lowercase, with the text cursor at the end, indicating it is being appended into the empty search field rather than replacing existing content. A dropdown autocomplete panel is open below the field, with the first suggestion reading "github - Google 搜索," and other suggestions including "GitHub" with the black cat logo and entries such as "github copilot," "github desktop," "github login," and "github trending." In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open on the Google homepage in Chinese, with the Google logo centered, Gmail and 图片 links at the upper right, and the suggestions list expanded under the search box.
caption.think: The user is entering the query GITHUB into Google's search field in order to search for GitHub.
caption.action: Type "GITHUB" into the Google search box.
caption.expectation: The search field should contain the completed query and the autocomplete suggestions should update to match GitHub-related results.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X placed on a plain light-gray horizontal area with no visible button, icon, text, handle, or input field, indicating a click on a blank region rather than a specific control. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is on the Google homepage with the query "github" in the central search box and the autocomplete dropdown open below it. The suggestions list includes "github - Google 搜索" and a highlighted GitHub suggestion with the black Octocat logo and the label "GitHub 公司." The click target is not on the suggestion list or search field, but on empty page background outside the dropdown.
caption.think: The user likely wants to dismiss the autocomplete suggestions while keeping the typed query in the search box, possibly to continue with a cleaner page state before the next action.
caption.action: Click the blank page background outside the search suggestions dropdown.
caption.expectation: The autocomplete dropdown should close or lose focus, while the typed query "github" remains in the Google search box.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the purple underlined search-result title link reading "GitHub · Change is constant. GitHub keeps you ahead. · GitHub." This is a clickable web page object, not a blank area or toolbar control. In the full-screen view, Chrome is displaying Google search results for "github," with the official GitHub result at the top of the results list and the clicked title belonging to that first result.
caption.think: The user likely wants to open the official GitHub website from the Google results page by selecting the top result.
caption.action: Click the top search-result title link "GitHub · Change is constant. GitHub keeps you ahead. · GitHub."
caption.expectation: The browser should navigate from Google results to the GitHub website, loading the selected result's page.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the green rounded rectangular button labeled "New," with a small white repository-style icon at the left of the text. This is a UI control, not a blank area. In the full-screen view, the button is in the left sidebar of the GitHub Dashboard page, above the "Top repositories" list and beside the repository search box.
caption.think: The user likely wants to start creating a new GitHub item from the dashboard, most likely a new repository, by pressing the clearly labeled New button.
caption.action: Click the green "New" button in the left sidebar.
caption.expectation: GitHub should open the creation flow for a new item, typically navigating to the new repository page or showing the new-item setup screen.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered inside the empty text input outlined in blue under the label "Repository name *". This is a UI control input field, not a blank region. In the full-screen view, Chrome is on GitHub's "Create a new repository" page, with the owner dropdown set to "lzy-young" and the repository name field active near the top of the form.
caption.think: The user likely wants to place focus in the required repository name field so they can enter the new repository's name.
caption.action: Click the empty "Repository name *" text field.
caption.expectation: The repository name field should gain focus with a text caret inside it, ready for typing.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The visible text entry area is the blue-outlined input field labeled "Repository name *" in GitHub's "Create a new repository" form. The field currently contains the text "test," indicating the repository name box is active and the new typing will append to the existing content rather than replace it. In the full-screen view, Chrome is open to github.com/new, with owner set to "lzy-young," a green availability message under the field, and the rest of the repository setup options below.
caption.think: The user is entering the desired repository name by continuing to type in the focused repository name field, changing the name from "test" to "testTEST1" if appended literally.
caption.action: Type "TEST1" into the focused "Repository name *" field, appending it to the existing text.
caption.expectation: The repository name field should update to include the newly typed text, and GitHub should immediately re-check and display availability or validation for the updated repository name.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered inside the empty multiline text box labeled "Description." The box has a blue focus outline, indicating it is a standard text input control in the form. In the full-screen view, Chrome is on GitHub's "Create a new repository" page; the owner is set to "lzy-young," the repository name field contains "test1" with a green availability message "test1 is available.," and the Description field sits just above the "Configuration" section.
caption.think: After entering the repository name, the user likely wants to place the cursor in the optional Description field to begin entering repository details.
caption.expectation: The Description field should gain focus with a text caret inside it, ready for typing.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the active single-line text field labeled "Description" with a blue focus outline. The field already contains the text "test1 is for tes", so the new typing will append to the existing description rather than replace it. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open to GitHub's "Create a new repository" page; the owner is "lzy-young," the "Repository name *" field contains "test1" with a green "test1 is available." message, and the Description field sits above the "Configuration" section.
caption.think: The user is finishing the repository description by appending the remaining characters so the full description reads "test1 is for test."
caption.action: Type "TEST1 IS FOR TEST" into the focused "Description" field, appending to the current text.
caption.expectation: The Description field should update immediately with the newly typed text, and the character count below it should increase accordingly.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in view shows the GitHub "Create a new repository" form with the blue-outlined "Description" field containing the complete text "test1 is for test" and the "Configuration" section immediately below it, including cards for "Choose visibility," "Add README," ".gitignore," and "Add license." There is no semitransparent red X, which matches a scroll action rather than a click. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open on github.com/new, the owner is "lzy-young," the repository name is "test1" with a green "test1 is available." message, and the page scrollbar indicates the page can be moved further downward.
caption.think: The user has finished entering the repository description and now wants to move down the GitHub new-repository page to reach lower configuration options or the repository creation controls.
caption.action: Scroll down on the main page.
caption.expectation: The page should move downward, bringing lower sections of the repository setup form further into view while the current top fields move upward.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X placed directly over the small gray toggle switch labeled "Off" in the "Add README" row. This is a UI control, specifically a binary on/off switch, not a blank area. Below it is the "No .gitignore" dropdown. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is on GitHub's "Create a new repository" page, with the repository name "test1," description "test1 is for test," and the Configuration section visible, including visibility, Add README, Add .gitignore, and Add license options.
caption.think: The user likely wants to enable the README option so the new repository will be initialized with a README file.
caption.action: Click the gray "Off" toggle switch in the "Add README" row.
caption.expectation: The toggle should switch to the enabled state, visually changing from "Off" to "On" and indicating that a README will be added to the new repository.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The crop shows the full GitHub form area without any semitransparent red X, which indicates a scroll action rather than selecting a specific control. Visible sections include "General" with owner "lzy-young," repository name "test1," the description field containing "test1 is for test," and the "Configuration" cards showing visibility set to "Public," the "Add README" toggle switched On, ".gitignore" set to "No .gitignore," and license set to "No license." At the bottom edge, the next section heading, "Jumpstart your project with Copilot (optional)," is partially visible. In the full-screen view, Chrome is open on GitHub's "Create a new repository" page, and the page scrollbar shows there is more content below.
caption.think: The user likely wants to continue moving down the repository creation page to reach the lower options and eventually the repository creation button.
caption.action: Scroll down on the main page.
caption.expectation: The page should move downward, bringing more of the "Jumpstart your project with Copilot (optional)" section and the lower repository setup controls into view.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the gray dropdown control labeled "No .gitignore" with a small downward-pointing arrow on its right side. This is a UI control in the configuration form, specifically the selector for adding a .gitignore template. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open to GitHub's "New repository" page. The "Configuration" section is visible with "Public" selected, "Add README" switched On, ".gitignore" set to "No .gitignore," and "No license" below it. The target is the .gitignore dropdown control, not a blank region.
caption.think: The user likely wants to choose a .gitignore template for the new repository after enabling the README and scrolling to the configuration options.
caption.expectation: The .gitignore selector should open, showing a menu or list of available .gitignore templates to choose from.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows an open dropdown list for .gitignore templates. A semitransparent red X is centered on the list item labeled "Actionscript," which appears as a selectable row beneath "AL" and above "Ada." In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is on GitHub's "New repository" page in the Configuration section. The ".gitignore" menu is expanded under the "Add .gitignore" row, with a search field at the top and options including "No .gitignore," "AL," "Actionscript," "Ada," and others.
caption.think: The user likely wants to choose the Actionscript .gitignore template for the new repository.
caption.action: Click the "Actionscript" option in the .gitignore dropdown list.
caption.expectation: The dropdown should close, and the .gitignore selector should update from "No .gitignore" to "Actionscript," indicating that template is selected.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a gray dropdown button labeled "No license" with a small downward-pointing arrow on the right. A semitransparent red X is centered directly on this dropdown, indicating the exact click target. This is a UI control, not a blank area or canvas object. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open on GitHub's "New repository" page in the Configuration section, where ".gitignore" is already set to "Actionscript" and the "Add license" row is visible just above the "Jumpstart your project with Copilot (optional)" section.
caption.think: The user likely wants to open the license selector next, after already choosing the .gitignore template, so they can pick a repository license.
caption.expectation: The license dropdown menu should open, showing available license options for selection.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows an open license dropdown titled "Choose a license" with a search field labeled "Search licenses" at the top. A semitransparent red X is centered on the list item labeled "Apache License 2.0," which is a selectable menu row in the dropdown. Nearby options include "No license," "GNU General Public License v3.0," and "MIT License." In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is on GitHub's "New repository" page in the Configuration section, and the "Add license" menu is expanded beneath the current "No license" selector.
caption.think: The user likely wants to select Apache License 2.0 as the repository license from the open license picker.
caption.action: Click the "Apache License 2.0" option in the open license dropdown menu.
caption.expectation: The dropdown should close, and the "Add license" selector should update to "Apache License 2.0," confirming the license choice.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the central portion of GitHub's New repository page with no red X, indicating a scroll action rather than a click target. Visible sections include General with the repository name set to "test1" and description "test1 is for test," followed by Configuration where visibility is set to "Public," README is toggled on, .gitignore is set to "Actionscript," and license is set to "Apache License 2.0." Below that, the next section, "Jumpstart your project with Copilot (optional)," is partially visible with the beginning of the Prompt area appearing near the bottom. In the full-screen view, Chrome is displaying github.com/new, and the page scrollbar is positioned partway down the page, consistent with moving further downward through the form.
caption.think: The user has finished choosing the repository setup options and likely wants to continue down the New repository form to reach the remaining sections and eventually the create repository controls.
caption.action: Scroll down on the GitHub New repository page.
caption.expectation: The page should move downward, bringing more of the lower sections of the repository creation form into view, likely revealing additional optional settings and the repository creation area.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a green rounded rectangular button labeled "Create repository" with white text. A semitransparent red X is centered directly on this button, so the target is that UI control. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is on GitHub's "New repository" page near the bottom of the form. Above the button is the "Jumpstart your project with Copilot (optional)" section with a large prompt text box, and the footer links are visible below, indicating this is the final submission area of the page.
caption.think: The user has finished configuring the new repository options and now wants to submit the form to create the repository.
caption.expectation: The button press should submit the repository creation form, and GitHub should begin creating the repository and navigate to the new repository page or loading state.
